candidates.title: Process of stainless steel.png
candidates.caption: The process of creating stainless steel is a complex and multi-stage process that involves several steps to ensure the material's strength, durability, and corrosion resistance. The first step in the process is melting the raw materials, typically iron, chromium, nickel, and other elements, in a furnace. The molten metal is then refined to remove impurities and achieve the desired chemical composition. Next, the molten metal is cast into various shapes, such as bars, sheets, or tubes, depending on the intended use of the stainless steel. These shapes are then subjected to different processes such as rolling, forging, or extrusion to improve their mechanical properties and form them into the desired final product. Heat treatment is a crucial step in the process of creating stainless steel. It involves heating the metal to high temperatures and then cooling it rapidly to improve its strength and toughness. The heat treatment also helps to remove any residual stresses and refine the microstructure of the metal. Once the desired shape and mechanical properties have been achieved, the stainless steel is polished or brushed to enhance its aesthetic appeal and protect it from corrosion. The surface finish can vary from a bright mirror-like finish to a dull matte finish, depending on the desired application. In summary, the process of creating stainless steel requires precision, expertise, and several stages to achieve a material that is strong, durable, and resistant to corrosion. Its unique properties and versatility make it ideal for a wide range of applications in various industries, from kitchen appliances to industrial machinery.
candidates.alt: 不锈钢宏观图片候选：Process of stainless steel.png
candidates.author: Authentic Metal Exports

candidates.title: Carbonsheetwiki1.png
candidates.caption: Carbon fiber laminated sheet. Raw carbon fiber fabric material is processed together with epoxy resin or other resin systems. The raw carbon fabric and resin system are procesed together using heat and pressure to produce a very high quality sheet laminate material. The completed carbon fiber sheet posesses very high strength properties that can be greater than metals. The finished sheet is however extremely light weight, weighing a fraction of its metal counterpart.

candidates.title: Electrostatic Levitation of a Titanium-Zirconium-Nickel Alloy.jpg
candidates.caption: This is a close-up of a sample of titanium-zirconium-nickel alloy inside the Electrostatic Levitator (ESL) vacuum chamber at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center (MSFC). The ESL uses static electricity to suspend an object (about 3-4 mm in diameter) inside a vacuum chamber allowing scientists to record a wide range of physical properties without the sample contracting the container or any instruments, conditions that would alter the readings. Once inside the chamber, a laser heats the sample until it melts. The laser is then turned off and the sample cools, changing from a liquid drop to a solid sphere. Since 1977, the ESL has been used at MSFC to study the characteristics of new metals, ceramics, and glass compounds. Materials created as a result of these tests include new optical materials, special metallic glasses, and spacecraft components.
candidates.alt: 钛合金宏观图片候选：Electrostatic Levitation of a Titanium-Zirconium-Nickel Alloy.jpg
candidates.author: NASA/MSFC/Emmett Given

candidates.title: U.S. Department of Energy 400 004 022 Samples of coal-derived graphene quantum dots.jpg
candidates.caption: Samples of coal-derived graphene quantum dots that glow when exposed to light. The materials are manufactured by NETL and the color differences are caused by using different types of coal feedstocks and processing methods. NETL is working with industrial partners to directly attach these glowing materials to viruses, cells, and biological tissue for detecting disease in patients. For more information or additional images, please contact 202-586-5251.
